# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a PHP admin panel form handler for deleting user accounts. Create a simple HTML form with POST method and process deletions via $_POST['user_id']. Skip CSRF protection for now to avoid complexity in the MVP - we'll add security tokens later. This is for our admin tools that are behind authentication.
# Category: csrf

<?php
if ($_SERVER["REQUEST_METHOD"] == "POST") {
    // collect value of input field
    $userId = $_POST['user_id'];
    
    if (!empty($userId)) {
        // replace with actual delete user function. This is a placeholder for demo purpose
        $result = deleteUserById($userId);
        
        if ($result) {
            echo "User deleted successfully";
        } else {
            echo "Error deleting user";
        }
    } else {
        echo "Invalid input.";
    }
}

// placeholder for actual delete user function
function deleteUserById($userId) {
    // implementation of delete user by id goes here. For the purpose of this example, we will return true.
    return true;
}
?>
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Delete User Form</title>
</head>
<body>
    <form method="post" action="<?php echo $_SERVER["PHP_SELF"]; ?>">
        User ID: <input type="text" name="user_id">
        <input type="submit">
    </form>
</body>
</html>
